title: Mail timestamps show UTC instead of local timezone
description:
Mail messages display timestamps in UTC instead of user's local timezone.

**Observed:**
- Message shows: 2026-01-12T13:18:39.916149+00:00
- User is in Israel (Asia/Jerusalem, UTC+2)
- Should display: 2026-01-12T15:18:39 (local time)

**Affected:**
- agent_mail_client.py inbox/read commands
- Possibly mail_processor.py alerts

**Fix:**
- Detect local timezone from system or .env
- Convert UTC timestamps to local time for display

title: Telegram: calendar event requests duration bug
description:
Bug: creating new calendar events from Telegram requests always uses 1h duration regardless of requested duration. Also the confirmation request screen does not indicate duration (or end time), so user cannot verify before confirming.

Expected: event duration matches request (start+end or duration), and confirmation screen shows duration (and/or end time).

Repro: Create a new calendar event request in Telegram with duration != 1h; observe created event is 1h and confirmation screen lacks duration.

Notes: Repo has no GitHub issues currently; track in Beads for multi-session fix.

description:

**Goal:**

- Use SQLite (.db) for performance/features, gitignored locally.
- Ensure root agent can access it via `bd --db` flag.

**Details:**

- Configure local context to use this DB.
- Ensure `*.db` is ignored in global1sim.
- Verify access: `bd --db Projects/global1sim/.beads/beads.db list`.

Review: vibranium-sync.sh likely obsolete
description:
## Finding

`Scripts/Orchestration/vibranium-sync.sh` appears obsolete due to hooks/triggers replacing its functionality.

## Analysis

| vibranium-sync.sh Step | Current Replacement |
|------------------------|---------------------|
| Auto-commit local changes | Manual (intentional) |
| Run sync.sh | `bd sync` + MCP Agent Mail |
| Remote-update --force | Git hooks + Agent Mail coordination |
| Verify git status | Pre-commit hooks |

## Script Location
`Scripts/Orchestration/vibranium-sync.sh` (Mac-specific, hardcoded to /Users/macbook/Documents/Unified_System)

## Recommendation
- Archive or delete the script
- 'Vibranium Mode' concept (Phase 2 TokenBroker/encryption) remains valid
- Only the sync script is redundant

## Action Required
Igor agents to verify and decide: archive, delete, or keep for edge cases.

---
*Reported by VioletCastle*

title: Epic: Argon2id Key Derivation Migration
description:
## Overview
Migrate TokenBroker key derivation from PBKDF2 to Argon2id for improved resistance against GPU/ASIC brute-force attacks.

## Why Argon2id
- Memory-hard: requires significant RAM, defeating parallel attacks
- Side-channel resistant: hybrid mode combines Argon2i + Argon2d
- Industry standard: used by 1Password, Bitwarden, libsodium
- PHC winner (2015), recommended by OWASP

## Complexity Cost
- New dependency: argon2-cffi (~50KB)
- Build: needs C compiler or pre-built wheel
- Code change: ~20 lines in token_broker.py
- Testing: verify on Linux, macOS, Windows

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] argon2-cffi added to dependencies
- [ ] TokenBroker uses Argon2id for new vaults
- [ ] PBKDF2 fallback for existing vaults (migration path)
- [ ] Tests pass on all platforms
- [ ] Documentation updated

title: Add argon2-cffi dependency
description: Add argon2-cffi to pyproject.toml/requirements.txt. Verify wheel availability for Linux/macOS/Windows.

title: Implement Argon2id in TokenBroker
description: Update token_broker.py to use Argon2id for key derivation. Keep PBKDF2 fallback for existing vaults. Parameters: memory=65536KB, iterations=3, parallelism=4.

title: Add vault migration utility
description: Create Scripts/Utilities/migrate_vault.py to re-encrypt existing PBKDF2 vaults with Argon2id. Include --dry-run and --backup options.

title: Test Argon2id on all platforms
description: Verify argon2-cffi installs and works correctly on: Linux (x86_64), macOS (arm64), Windows. Add CI test if applicable.

title: [COMM-ARCH] Evaluate Agno framework for provider-agnostic agent communication
description:
Investigate using Agno (or similar) to abstract away provider dependencies in agent communication layer.

**Context:**
Current implementation tightly couples to specific providers (MCP server, Telegram). If we want to swap providers (e.g., different LLM backends, different notification channels), we need significant rework.

**Concerns:**
- Provider lock-in (MCP server specific, Telegram-only alerts)
- Can we use other providers? (Slack, Discord, email, SMS, webhooks)
- What about different LLM backends? (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, local models)

**Ideas to explore:**
1. **Agno framework** - evaluate if it provides provider abstraction layer
2. **Plugin architecture** - notification plugins (Telegram, Slack, email, webhook)
3. **Provider interface** - define abstract interface, implement per-provider adapters
4. **Message broker** - consider Redis/RabbitMQ/NATS for decoupled pub/sub
5. **OpenTelemetry-style approach** - exporters pattern for notifications

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Document current provider dependencies
- Evaluate Agno capabilities for our use case
- Propose abstraction layer design (if Agno doesn't fit)
- Ensure backward compatibility with existing MCP Mail + Telegram setup
acceptance_criteria:
- Documented current provider dependencies
- Agno evaluation complete with pros/cons
- Abstraction design proposed
- Migration path defined (if applicable)

title: Add list_agents endpoint to MCP Agent Mail server
description:
## Problem
Server has no `list_agents` endpoint. Client works around by checking known names via `whois()`, but cannot discover dynamically registered agents.

## Solution
Add `list_agents` tool to MCP Agent Mail server that returns all registered agents for a project.

## API Design
```json
{
  "tool": "list_agents",
  "arguments": {
    "project_key": "/home/gonya/Unified_System"
  }
}
```

Returns:
```json
{
  "agents": [
    {"name": "VioletCastle", "program": "claude-code", "last_active": "..."},
    {"name": "OrangeStone", "program": "antigravity-core", "last_active": "..."}
  ]
}
```

## Dependencies
- US-27a.4 (closed) - Current workaround implemented
- Requires access to mcp_agent_mail server codebase

## Sub-tasks
1. Add list_agents tool to server
2. Update agent_mail_client.py to use native list_agents
3. Remove hardcoded agent list workaround
4. Update AGENT_MAIL_QUICKSTART.md

title: Frequent beads sync merge conflicts resurrecting deleted issues
description:
During bd sync, merge conflicts frequently occur causing deleted issues to be resurrected.

**Observed behavior:**
- Sync reports 'removed N deleted issue(s) that were resurrected by git merge'
- This session saw 8 issues resurrected: US-1tm.1, US-1tm.2, US-1tm.3, US-27a.1, US-27a.2, US-27a.3, US-m3i, US-qir
- Rebase conflicts in beads-sync worktree require manual intervention (--skip)

**Expected behavior:**
- Sync should handle concurrent edits gracefully without resurrecting deleted issues
- No manual rebase intervention needed

**Root cause investigation needed:**
- Check beads-sync worktree rebase strategy
- Investigate JSONL merge conflict resolution
- Consider using merge instead of rebase, or implementing proper 3-way merge for JSONL
notes: Investigated: bd 0.36.0 already implements deletion tracking via deletions.jsonl and 'Protecting N issue(s)' mechanism during sync. Manual rebase --skip no longer required. Resolved.

title: Tailscale1 interface unreachable on code-minion - iptables blocking tailscale1
description:
## Problem
Users cannot ping or SSH to code-minion via tailscale1 interface.

## Root Cause
iptables ts-input chain rule: -A ts-input -s 100.64.0.0/10 ! -i tailscale0 -j DROP
Dropped all Tailscale CGNAT traffic not from tailscale0.

## Resolution
1. Added: iptables -I ts-input 3 -i tailscale1 -j ACCEPT
2. Added ts-forward rules for tailscale1
3. Created /etc/systemd/system/tailscale-iptables-fix.service

title: [TOOL] Phone Interface: Human-Agent Voice/Interactive Layer
description:
Create a 'Phone' tool/interface for direct real-time interaction between agents and humans.

**Concept:**
A bridge allowing agents to 'call' or 'message' a human operator through interactive voice or structured interactive UI (beyond simple text/mail).

**Capabilities:**
- Voice-to-Text/Text-to-Voice (VAPI/Twilio/ElevenLabs integration)
- Interactive Push Notifications with action buttons
- 'Call' priority: high-latency (mail) vs low-latency (phone)
- Agent persona awareness (VioletCastle calls from Kosta's laptop)

**Ideas to explore:**
1. **VAPI/Twilio Integration** - for actual voice calls.
2. **Interactive Push (Firebase/OneSignal)** - for quick action confirmations.
3. **Escalation Protocol** - when mail processor alerts aren't enough, 'call' the human.
4. **Agno Abstraction** - ensure Phone is just another provider in the Agno model.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Define 'Phone' tool interface in AGENTS.md
- Proof-of-Concept: Send interactive push or triggered voice call
- Integrate with existing escalation rules in AGENT_COMMUNICATION_GUIDE.md
acceptance_criteria: - Phone tool interface defined\n- POC interactive call/push successful\n- Escalation logic integrated
